reviews.review_text:
I genuinely loved V Rising.
The combat, castle building, progression, PvP — all of it is incredibly well made, and I put hundreds of hours into the game because I truly enjoyed it.

Which is exactly why this feels so awful.

I was permanently banned from official servers, not for cheating myself, but because another random player I happened to interact with was apparently cheating. Someone I never played with before or after. According to support, simply continuing to play around that situation counted as “benefiting” from cheats.

What frustrates me most is that there seems to be no room for context, nuance, or intent. Competitive PvP servers are chaotic. People accuse each other of cheating constantly, joke around in chat constantly, and temporary alliances or interactions happen all the time — especially when entire servers are dominated by massive groups teaming together.

I never used ESP or any cheat software. Not once.

Yet I ended up permanently banned across all official servers anyway.

What hurts is that I actually respected the game and the time I invested into it. I even tried communicating respectfully with support throughout the process because I genuinely wanted to resolve it fairly.

I still think V Rising is an amazing game mechanically. But after this experience, I honestly feel discouraged from investing time into official PvP again if association alone can lead to a permanent ban without clear proof of direct cheating.

I’m not writing this out of hatred for the game. I’m writing it because I genuinely cared about it, and this whole situation has been incredibly frustrating and disappointing.
Great game. vampire fantasy largely, cool building mechanics, fun combat and boss fights, pve and pvp. One of my favourite games.

V Rising honestly is one of the best survival experiences out there, in my opinion. It kind of builds on the usual foundations you already know from the genre but it also has this strong identity, the atmosphere, and a very clear dedication to the vampire fantasy so it never really feels like yet another copy, in a sea of overcrowded releases. In a genre where a lot of titles blend together, V Rising kind of slips through and finds its own spot, and it does it in a way that leaves a mark, for real.
[hr][/hr]
[h2]Atmosphere & Identity[/h2]

What really, like instantly drew me into V Rising was the atmosphere. The game kind of fully commits to its gothic vampire vibe, building a world that feels elegant, dark, and honestly pretty immersive. Even the way the architecture sits, the environments look, and how the enemies are designed, it all adds up. Like, every little part helps the fantasy of being a rising vampire lord, not just another survivor wandering through some harsh world. Every region, every castle, even the enemies they all stack up into that fantasy of becoming a rising vampire lord, not merely another survivor running around, grabbing resources in some hostile place. And more, it really seems to understand who it is. A lot of survival games talk about freedom, but then they end up making you feel kinda aimless, like ok…now what?. V Rising goes another route, because it keeps throwing you a clear sense of purpose and steady progression, almost all the time.
[hr][/hr]
[h2]Progression & Systems[/h2]

The progression system is honestly one of the stronger parts, like it really keeps pulling you forward. Each boss isn’t just… you know, another obstacle on the way. when you beat them you get worthwhile rewards that expand what you can do right away, and also push you into new directions. So it feels like this steady loop where every win matters, every little advance feels earned, not like it was handed to you. More importantly, most players almost always know what they’re supposed to do next and how to get there. So instead of spending all day digging through guides, and wikis, you can actually spend your time playing the game.
Also, the boss lineup is a great. They each bring their own vibe themes, mechanics, and combat rhythm, so progression doesn’t turn into the same routine over and over. The game keeps dropping fresh kinds of trouble, which means the whole experience stays interesting from beginning till the end, even when you think you’ve seen it all. And beyond fighting, the systems for prisoners and servants really add to the vampire fantasy. It’s not only about surviving anymore. You’re building influence, organizing power, and setting control over what’s happening around you. That makes you feel like an actual vampire ruler, which is basically exactly the goal the game is aiming for. Then there’s the building system, which honestly deserves credit too. Sure, it’s simpler than a lot of other survival games, but that simplicity works in its favor. It’s smooth to use, efficient, and it doesn’t usually turn into some annoying grind of frustration. 
[hr][/hr]
[h2]Boss Design & Endgame Frustrations[/h2]

That said, the game is not without a few flaws, it kind of shows. Some of the endgame bosses felt frustrating though, not really because they were straight up difficult. It was more that a number of these encounters seem to lean on mechanics, and odd little gimmicks that occasionally feel kind of disconnected from the boss overall design. Instead of actually testing mastery of the game combat systems, those moments sometimes manufacture frustration for reasons that feel… sort of artificial. Not the nice kind of challenge, the kind that you can’t quite tie back to anything. A tough boss can be incredibly satisfying, no question. But a frustrating boss leaves, a totally different, impression in your head.
[hr][/hr]
[h2]The Endgame Grind[/h2]

My biggest criticism, however, kinda lies with the endgame grind. I get that resource gathering is a basic part of the survival genre , and I dont really mind grinding when it feels meaningful. The issue is that a lot of the endgame asks players to keep repeatedly gathering resources they have already farmed like a hundred times throughout the whole game . At that point it stops feeling like real progression and starts feeling more like a chore , you know? What used to be engaging slowly turns into something repetitive, and then the pacing kinda suffers because of it.
[hr][/hr]
[h2]Music[/h2]

One small thing I kinda wish the game had done differently is how it handles major boss themes. The soundtrack is fine, but honestly I would have loved unique musical themes for the most important encounters, like not just the same overall vibe. At the very least, the final boss of each act could have had its own signature track. Memorable music has this weird way to elevate the fights and stick in your head long after the battle ends. A special theme for those key moments could have made several of the great bosses feel truly unforgettable, like Simon Belmont, for example.
[hr][/hr]
[h2]Final Thoughts[/h2]

Despite a few frustrations with some boss mechanics, and the sheer amount of grinding that keeps popping up in the later parts of the game, V Rising stays a pretty exceptional experience.  
The whole atmosphere is captivating, the progression feels consistently rewarding, and the systems somehow just fit together like they belong there, to deliver one of the best survival experince out there. Very few survival games manage to balance approachability, real depth, and a clear identity, and as far as I can tell V Rising does.
9.5/10

V Rising was one of those games I bought either early on or on sale and shelved it for later. Well here we are 2 years later roughly finally getting around to trying the game, and I can honestly say its great. The combat feels nice, base building goes crazy, theres some resource management but its not extreme like some other titles would be. I am solo with 20+ hrs into my playthrough and am not even done with Act 2 of 4. If your thinking of getting this I would highly recommend it whether on sale or not! I will add that the developer is done dropping content for the game at this time of review.
Fun to play with friends, but be ready for a lot of grinding :P

I'm not usually into Survival games, but I've been obsessed since I got it. You play as a vampire slowly gaining power and building your own castle to become the next Dracula. You hunt down bosses to steal their powers, and each one is unique with new mechanics that you must learn to survive. There are many weapons to unlock each providing a basic, special, and special plus attack, they can we swapped on the fly for incredible and addictive combat. There is incredible visual story telling with roaming monsters and bosses who might just ambush and even gang up on you. You'll need to be clever and determined but every second will be fun!

Pretty decent survival/action rpg about vampires where you can build your own castle, the game has some balancing issues mostly with an endgame boss, megara, which ended up beign harder to beat than the last boss of the game, even when using a blood type that reduces the damage she does. Or the grinding part for materials to be able to craft late game stuff, the game gets stingy with the drops of some of the materials, luckily if you are playing alone or you are the host/admin you can modify the drop rate and make it more bearable if you start to get annoyed. Otherwise its pretty solid and fun, more if you play with a friend!

very good and addicting with some interesting mechanics, can be a bit frustrating with a few bosses but on the other hand there are bosses that are really fun to go against, overall a very good Survival experience 9.5/10.

works for me
base building
challenging PvE bosses
and most important, fun PvP

I genuinely loved the game and played many hours with my friends and we built pretty castles together I had fun fighting the bosses and all but the server we were on got shut down or deleted idk not sure and there is no way to get your progress back I would've made my own server if I could but it's too confusing to make a "dedicated server" and also with that the game deletes them after a couple of months I've learned 
I wish it could've worked more like bg3 that it's just based on one person saving the game and others can join makes life easier and nothing ever gets deleted so even if I want to come back after a year I can 

Other than the terrible saving system the game was good so it really breaks my heart to lose everything
It was worth every second, i wish i could forget it and start over.

one of those games where you start playing and suddenly 12 hours have gone by, such a fun game with easy to learn mechanics and it’s visually stunning to look at. i bought it with zero expectations that i would enjoy it as much as i have but im hooked and i can’t wait to see what more the devs come up with :)

Favorite survivalcraft game.
I love the world so much. Most of the time whenever I play these kinds of games, the world feels so empty or dead but populated solely for the player. 
I love how villagers can either freeze in fear or try to square up against you.
I love how patrols of opposing factions other than your own will fight each other in the streets.
I love how you can find workers tinkering with lightning harvester towers.
Very minor stuff but they added so much life into a world and I hope other games see this and do the same.

Combat is so fun. Top down but camera can be somewhat-freely moved. I've put several hours into games like Diablo, Final Fantasy XIV, and Lost Ark, and this games grabs those and makes a survivalcraft out of them. As a melee player, I went back and forth between blood, unholy, and static, then ended up going full-on blood for the theming. 
Boss fights are so much fun! My only advise is to google on how to increase their HP pools, so that you don't kill them as fast. Or just play on Brutal difficulty! There's a few settings you can't tweak in game but can via their json files.

Builds are fun, you can mix or match. I've avoided searching meta guides like I usually do when it comes to action games, so I can't say I have mathematical experience on testing builds, but it's really nice to be able to mix and match builds and still do well. I've beaten the final boss with pure blood build, hybrid blood/necro, and hybrid blood/static. (I just really love builds that match the blood theme). It's definitely harder as a melee but it's not impossible!

Castle.......my beloved. Tile based (like huge tiles, not 1x1 size that you might be used to) and the games' machines/facilities push you to try and make separate rooms for different kinds of facilities to get bonuses like faster craft rates and cheaper craft costs. You can make several floors and mid to late game ish the game gives you in-castle teleporters so that you can get to your 5th floor very easily.
Servants exist and are basically your type of horizontal progression where you gear them enough to be able to take on zones that gives you resources. Resource generation!

Overall this game is great, I wish I tried it sooner, the free weekend really made me jump into it and I bought it within finishing the first biome.
I'm really sad that it seems like the devs are done with this game, I could totally see more improvements being made like certain QoL like redeploying servants to the same area or even hitting lvl 100, but I will still look forward to their already-confirmed next project of which we don't know much about yet. I will be there day 1 this time!

Probably the best vampire game out there atm (to which I believe there’s sadly a distinct lack of good ones). The boss fights are incredibly well made, the combat feels very fluid, the base building looks incredible, and the progression is perfect. You don’t realize just how much you learn while playing the game until you do a repeat playthrough and suddenly everything you once struggled with is clicking and you hit a perfect flow state in which you’re perfectly blocking/dodging nearly every attack. The blood mechanics are also really cool, granting you traits from the last person you fed on, which change based on the unit’s skills, species, and blood quality. Overall I’d give this game an easy 9.5/10, my only complaint is that there’s no difficulty between normal and brutal that has the brutal difficulty attack patterns without the incredibly punishing hits to practice, but that’s just a minor nitpick.
PVE is great, aside from siege golem and castle raiding which could destroy everything.

Layers of tedium and incredibly slow travel interrupted by clunky and poorly thought out combat. Stunlocks are rampant, your only defense is one dash every ten seconds, your base movespeed is agonizingly slow, and your offense is limited to two spells at a time when the game offers almost 50.
Outside of combat the game is completely ruined by a lack of basic QoL. You're constantly running around your house micromanaging servant inventories because nobody ever thought to just let you do it remotely. Then they turn around and block you from building for two minutes because there's no ability to move them out of the way. 
This game somehow fails to learn the incredibly basic lessons from any of the genres it's pilfering from. It's not even playable without extensively customizing default world settings, since the developers assumed it'd be played exclusively in PVP multiplayer when nobody actually wants that.

This is a really hard one for me to review. Its a bit of a mixed bag and while I DID (and still do) enjoy my time playing, I would have a hard time recommending it to others.

I love building up my castle but I very much dislike how much of the building progression is gated behind boss progression. In many cases this feels gamey and arbitrary, though at least they made an attempt at an in universe justification for this mechanic.

Movement and combat cool downs generally feel VERY slow. I spent some time playing with a friend and he made a comment about it feeling like his character was moving through molasses. The combat its self feels... passable in most cases but some of the bosses feel a bit cheesy and unfair in single player though I admit that this is probably a skill issue on my part. Enemies generally feel a bit spongier then one would expect but, again, this is probably a skill issue on my part. If I had to describe combat in one sentence, it would be "A worse version of World of Warcraft".

I was generally impressed with the environmental visuals though some of the humanoid models are REALLY bad. Like, "Why is this dude's neck half the length of his torso" levels of bad.

The soundtrack is fairly good, with a few standout tracks but otherwise nothing too special. I particularly liked the arrangement of Bloody Tears form the Legacy of Castlevania DLC.

Story wise, there's not really much to say. This is primarily and action game trying to be an MMO, with all of the games overarching plot being given in the opening movie. You can get little tidbits of context if you read the boss description but that's about it.

Oh, and it seems that most of the default setting (combat, resource management, etc.) are tuned for multi-player. If you are intending to play the game single player, with default settings, it CAN be done, but will feel very slow and grindy. 

Also it seems to be impossible to play with Steam in offline mode. The game seems to require some sort of server authentication every 4 to 5 minuets even if you are playing single-player. I consider that a major no-no.
This game is so much fun and it was worth every cent!! I have teamed up with my friend online and slowly we will progress until we rise again and become the Vampy Overlords we once were!!

This is such a fun and unique game.
The great thing about V Rising is that it does some simple stuff that make every activity enjoyable- even stuff that are normally not "yout jam":

[b] Combat [/b] - Improved action arpg fun. Melee combat feels better than genre defining staples (Diablo 4 who?), a lot of weapon type choices each with unique skills. Spellcasting is like skill shots taken with Moba like precision, and just the fact the you are not "class" locked and can mix and match gives so much freedom, personalization and skill expression. Its simply great.

[b] Lore [/b] - Not through dialouge, but through atmosphere and memorable encounters that build a world. It is not sugar coating vampire fantasy- you are the elite prededator that tries to rebuild your empire.
You like Vampire fantasy? Well, this game does not appologize about vampire nature, it owns it. Simple- therefore great.

[b]Survival Elements [/b] - this is NOT my thing, and this is why I was late to the party. It is very much a survival game, you need to gather materials, craft and build. Your entire progression is tied only to your gear level and not exp grinding (I wish I could just grind because the combat is fun). BUT mining/ harvesting is not "interacting"- it's beating the **** out of a rock / tree / creature, and this is fun, because you can utilize your weapons and skills for it as well.
 Castle building there for showing off design skills / going wild / min maxing production, and each approach just gives so much satisfaction. I wasted hours on this, and it is Simsly great (and I hated Sims). 

[b] Progression [/b] - The right amount of hand holding vs mystery. I always know my HL goal, but you need to still figure out some things yourself to keep it interesting and rewarding. Simple, and great.

[b] Co op [/b] - Just a big W here. Private game / private server are both amazing options to play with friends, with your settings (allow teleport with any item, no weapon durability loss), in a pace that suits your partner / playgroup. When my gf want a break I can still farm for us, build, and explore.

[b] Online PVP [/b] - Can't tell you much here as we play private (2v2 clans with our friends has been a blast tho). It looks cool but I prefer to control my settings. Might try later.

The price is worth it, they don't charge triple A price tag and some triple A studios can learn a lot from this game design choices. [b] Pick it up. [/b]

Fine gameplay we have here. 60% - endless running to somewhere, 20% - grinding for something, 10% - bossfighting with clunky controls and battle mechanics.
Really sad that i tried to give a chance to it and didnt refund from start.

I haven’t played a game in a while that got me this addicted to progressing. I was never a huge ARPG guy — I loved Diablo 2, but other than that I was always more into Final Fantasy-type RPGs — so I didn’t expect to enjoy V Rising this much.

The building system is so damn good, honestly even more fun than The Sims. The game’s difficulty is also really nice. It’s not Soulslike hard or anything, but finally beating a V Blood boss after getting your ass kicked a few times and then extracting their blood feels satisfying as hell every time.

On top of that, the art style is amazing, and you can clearly tell the developers put a lot of care and passion into the game.

Overall, this game is really dope
A good social game, if you're on PvE.  Fun and intriguing gameplay, fast paced fun.

V Rising is honestly one of the most incredible games I’ve ever played.
Everything about it feels polished and unforgettable — the combat, the atmosphere, the progression, the base building, the music, and the entire vampire theme are executed almost perfectly.

I rarely find a game that keeps me fully invested for so long, but V Rising managed to do exactly that. It delivers a unique experience that feels fresh, addictive, and incredibly satisfying at the same time.

After playing it, I genuinely struggled to find another game that could compete with the same level of quality and enjoyment. For me, it sets a new standard for survival and action RPG games.

An absolute masterpiece.

In most games, gatekeeping progress is a big Hassle (in my opinion). However this game does it well in the sanse you can only progress by doing the quest line so there is no shortcut to the end game (with the exception of joining end game players donating everything to you if they want too).

It is very solo player friendly but more fun to play with friends.
VRising is THE benchmark for its niche. Great mechanics, awesome visual, fabulous thematics, hyper fair price and close to no bugs. I enjoy specially the PVE experience, it is a whole game on its on.

I bought this game and I played it for 2 hours, built a small castle in an online public server and closed the game. After 8 hours I open it again and my castle is just gone. Turns out I have to keep providing it with "blood essence" or something like that for it not to be destroyed. It's really frustrating although it's a fun game with a fun concept tbh. So I decided to refund it because I don't have time to keep checking on my castle every time and I don't want to play offline too, additionally, I don't really care to play with a team and ask them to protect my castle, because I don't have regular play time. So this game is not for me,

I understand that in an online server, to keep our castles forever it will be a mess, but at least increase the duration for several days or one week. But those games that require too much time investing are not for casual players like me,

This game's a lot of fun for a Survival/Base Building game. Off the bat I'd give it a 9/10 and encourage anyone considering it to pick it up.

The combat is fun similar to top down games like Grim Dawn or Path Of Exile. Each weapon starts fairly weak and as you upgrade them eventually get two skills such as throwing out a javelin and having it spin to return to you or dash attacks with the great sword. Each weapon has unique abilities, speeds of attack, and damage making for a unique experience with each one. Later they get enchantments that can apply magical effects like leeching their life, setting them on fire, or cursing them so on death they rise as undead. The armors, amulets and skills allow for several different builds like a necromancer, life leech, fire magic, ice magic, illusionist, and an additional build or two.

The bosses are likely to be the main draw for a lot of people to the game and they don't disappoint. Each boss plays a bit differently like a frost archer, vampires based around trapping you in an iron maiden and draining your blood, or nightmarish mutated monsters. Every time you kill them you unlock upgrades for your gear, castle, and companions in game. On brutal difficulty, the bosses also gain extra phases or attacks which makes a brutal run feel fresh if you've already played through it. Without spoiling too much Dracula is an awesome fight and while certain elements are bullethellish to some of the later game bosses including Dracula he's one of the most fun bosses I've killed in a game.

The castle building is awesome full stop. It's very easy to make something that looks good with the way pieces snap together and the aesthetics were designed with this in mind, so even if you make an "ugly stone shoebox" the interiors are very easy to light, decorate, and make look great. There are dozens of decorative textures to apply to interior and exterior walls, different furniture sets, and all kinds of plants and items to design any kind of vampiric fantasy castle you can imagine.

In regards to the survival elements of this game the survival can be as difficult, grindy, fast paced, or easy as you want it to be. You can modifier player health and damage, loot that drops, drop stacks, resources from your servants and so on even day and night length. The survival elements are also probably the best implemented I've seen in a Survival Game such that calling V Rising a Survival Game feels like a disservice. The sun is one of your primary foes in the early game and encourages players to only explore at night or in the shadows of trees (the game starts in a forest), so it makes it fun to explore and fight feeling that time limit you are always up against, eventually you get better capes, blood types and other ways to resist the influence of the sun. Blood types are a mechanic where enemies have different stat buffs they provide based on what kind of creature/fighter they are providing buffs to gathering resources, movement speed, crit damage, health leech, or spell power and cooldown. It makes eating not just a "mechanic you are forced to engage with" for the sake of the genre but something that feels believable and fun to do. Eventually you get prisons where you can kidnap people and drain their blood on tap to get your favorite buffs whenever you want them. 

The game is really good I've put almost 1000 hours in and played with several of my friends, check it out and if you made it this far thanks for reading.

absolutely terrible game, the games idea of difficulty is just spamming your screen with ♥♥♥♥♥, the last quarter of the game is filled with ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥♥, ♥♥♥♥ all over your screen and practically no way to dodge it, every boss fight summons their little chudlets to harass you like you're a cosplayer at an anime convention. Every aoe takes up the entire continent of America and the bosses teleport around as if you're playing against someone with 2000 ping, every single animation you want to do takes 5-7 business days and you stop whenever you're attacked. whoever greenlit this needs to take a long hard look in the mirror cos idk how they can sleep with themselves at night. the sun can ♥♥♥♥ off as well, how am i meant to find shade when the screen lights up like a damn 5 million lumen flashlight. don't even get me started on the movement speed, you move like a one legged geriatric hopped up on anaesthesia and then every attack that someone does puts a CC on you and the combat debuff makes you even slower. all the while paul the peasant with his pitchfork can ruin your entire day in seconds

You aren't a vampire in this game, you're a hot topic reject that got mangled in a car wreck

Wish I hadn't bought it. Least I got it on sale...

The game is tediously slow, and I mean everything is slow, traversal, combat and progression. And it's the latter that really stopped me in my tracks and made me realise how bad the design was. You hit progression like a wall, if you can't get past a boss for instance get your older brother to do it for you, because there's absolutely no way to get your character stronger without killing that boss. The biggest problem with this design I have is, you have to play the way the developers intended you to play. That's of course not inherently bad, but it does put a damper on your freedom to play the way you want to, which is the point of a game.

The game is too slow, no variety in abilities,  the game tries to squeeze a lot of hours by requiring boring activities, damage sponge bosses, killing enemies is not satisfying since the dropped items have no dopamine spikes it is always bones and stupid things, the base speed of the game is too slow (progression and movement speed, even in wolf form), lack of day activities and the teleport system is bad, why should I have no items in inventory to have the ability to teleport.
A fantastic look, farm and level up game. The Castle building mechanic is top tier. Buy this game.

I will never not glaze this game and everything (yes, everything) about it.

To make this review short: beautiful art direction, fun combat, great base building, and mechanics easy to pick up all backed by a wonderful team of devs. A very addicting experience and a very surprising gem in it's genre.

To make this review long:

I will preface by saying, one: I only ever play this game alone (odd, I know) or with friends- rarely do I play for pvp. Two: I was NEVER a fan of survival games or any kind of game that requires any amount of base building, maintenance, or management for that matter- despite how hard I would try. But V Rising has created a very effective and straightforward form of base building and packed the world with so much life and challenges that it's made the management so worth it- bearable and enjoyable, even. The optimization, resource gathering, crafting, leveling system, and base creation as well as it's defense is very fulfilling, then you get the thrill of venturing out, slaying to grow ever-more powerful. And slaying in general, you are a hot vampire after all.

Again, that's only an opinion on my end; as for the actual meat and potatoes, my personal favorite part: this game's combat. Is it hard and sweaty? Undoubtedly. But GOD is it worth it. Each V Blood is it's own challenge, you'll never find yourself fighting the same boss' move set twice, everything is unique in their arenas, design, and combat-- and as more and more content released during my time playing this game, I will never be disappointed, even through my rage. It's a satisfying feeling to have gotten to see this game's progression thus far.

I will admit, the power creep in [b]some[/b] stages has found itself to be just a little... unbalanced, but this rarely is an issue. There's a power level to meet or surpass for each V Blood ("bosses") in order for a fair fight; if you are a little off when trying to fight something of a higher level, it's difficult (duh), but it's still very doable, take it from a man who's attempted it many times. But it becomes especially apparent in newer, later acts [spoiler] Eg: The Oakveil [/spoiler] that the difficulty spike is greater than what I've delt with in early/mid game, but then it seems to level itself out once more. You will have to progress through the game by hunting and killing these different V Bloods in order to unlock reagents, magic, weapons, recipes, ect., continuing to reach different acts until you hit the final one and becoming your most powerful self. But as coined from the Metal Gear Online community: git gud. Where there's a will, there is always a way, there is always a build, and there is always a fun work-around to ♥♥♥♥ around if you believe all else fails. There are times where you may feel stuck in certain stages or that there's no way through or around it, but trust me: there is. The beauty is in refining yourself, fledgling.

There's so much I can put into this review, but I'll leave it this, and say: "grab the gang, get the game, and experience it for yourself." If I could beat these V Bloods for the very first time again, I would. If a picky bastard like me could finally experience a survival and base building game that is actually fun to play, I bet you would find something to fully enjoy about this game, too.

This game is an absolute blast. I’ve been playing with a few friends, and we’ve all completely lost track of time… which is fitting, because apparently vampires don’t sleep anyway.

The combat feels smooth and satisfying, and I love how each weapon completely changes your fighting style. One minute you’re gracefully dueling like some ancient vampire lord, and the next you’re wildly swinging a mace around like you just woke up angry after missing your daily supply of Type O negative.

The spell system is also fantastic. There’s a ton of customization, and it really lets you create your own style of vampire chaos. Whether you want to be a dark mage, a fast assassin, or just a menace to every villager unfortunate enough to be outside after sunset, the game gives you the freedom to do it.

But honestly, the castle building might be the best part. The building and design system is one of the best I’ve seen in any survival game. My friends and I started by building a “small castle,” and somehow ended up creating a gothic monstrosity that looks like Dracula discovered Pinterest.

The atmosphere, music, progression, and overall polish are incredibly well done. It’s one of those games where you log in thinking you’ll play for an hour and suddenly the sun is coming up in real life too.

Highly recommend this game to anyone who enjoys survival crafting, action combat, or simply fulfilling their lifelong dream of becoming a well-organized immortal homeowner.

A masterclass of a game, honestly I have nothing to complain about. Great crafting, building, combat, no issues or bugs, and is very fun. One of the best survival games out there! I would play this multiplayer though if you can.

Hell yes. Vampires? Check.  Castle building & decoration? Check. Absolutely sick, fluid combat? F*cking check.

Rage inducing moments where you get absolutely wrecked and you develop a bloodthirsty taste for vengeance? Check.

Buy it, you know you want to.  You don't just want to, YOU NEED TO. Just DO it!

I personally wouldn’t have played this game if it hadn’t been free for the weekend, because I thought it was some kind of woke sheet game. 
But I installed it since you know it was free, and when I launched it for the first time i was honestly fascinated. 
It’s a really interesting game.
Yeah, you do have to grind a bit more, but it’s still very fun. 
Personally, I like that you can play solo or on official servers.
It has an interesting concept of building your own vampire castle, turning humans into vampires, and so on.
I can’t really say for sure what else you can do yet because I’ve only played it for about 4 hours, but those 4 hours completely blew me away.
It's worth to buy it fully priced or on discount
9/10

Tons of content if you enjoy combat, crafting and base building.  You can have a relaxing session of resource collection and building or a high intensity session of hunting down bosses depending on what you are in the mood for.  Loads of quality of life improvements since the early access days.

One of the best vampire games ever created, up there with franchises like Castlevania.

it can be a cozy game thanks to the farming and constructing a castle mechanics, but also a hard game with the pvp servers, or join forces with other vampires in a pve server, which is my experience.

the boss fights are a delight, specially the final boss which encapsulates all the things you learned through out your journey.

what else can i say that others already said.

game is already done in development and team said they're working on their new project, however, i still believe this game could have kept coming with more content, but that doesn't mean the game wasn't finished, go ahead, play this masterpiece !!

9.5/10

The game is super fun, especially if you enjoy open-world and survival games. The atmosphere is amazing and really makes you feel like you’re inside a vampire world. You get to explore, build your own castle, and upgrade your character and weapons in a way that keeps you playing for hours without getting bored. The game is a mix of survival, RPG, and fast-paced combat, and it becomes way better with friends .  I really loved it.

There is almost no way to get out of a fight without getting hit a few times. Not to mention getting around the map is a pain even without people. if you could doge more than once before a 10 second cooldown it would be a bit more manageable but the second you are out numbered by enemies your level or higher your toast. When it comes to travel it is the people you encounter not the sun. When you finally get a horse a light breeze will kick you off even upgraded. 

All in all its alright but get the other game that caught your eye you may have more fun that way.

the game is alright, there is just no choice in the armor. Yes you get spell points and you can chose spells etc but once you upgrade and can make the next level of armor its just one set to chose from. also the same with weaponry. you have a crossbow already then you can craft a bow wich is an actual downgrade. Open world is very restrictive, theres a lot of places you just cant access. The blood tracking system is pretty useless at times too. All in all the game just isnt something that i want to play to the end.
No pause in Single Player mode. That just doesn't fit my lifestyle.

I came back to V a couple of years after launch, and I'm really happy to see that they added so much content.

It's given me plenty of hours of farming, building, designing and build-optimising. Couldn't ask for a more.
I am not interested in the PVP element, so I can't speak to how it performs there.
DLC is a bit too expensive if I were to get all of it, so unfortunately I've only played the base game.
Someone said if you like RPG and vampires this is the game for you. Its like Terraria but 3D and dark fantasy.  Got hooked from the first 30 minutes.

Sooooo came in with no expectations... I Do Love Playing a Vampire or a Lich or a Death Knight as the main char so This looked up my alley. Shockingly they have done a LOT of things Really Right in this game. First of all Great Forest! Feels thick, natural & Full of surprises which is Great. Right now i have been playing solo but i have a buddy that i will restart with tonight. What an Awesome fighting system! Its not combo oriented but you can DEFINATELY discover combos. The game decides to not give you spells out the gate but instead weapons with diff uses and abilities which is cool with me, u do launch technically with 2 spells and Both are Helpful and cool! Since you  have  to build a castle yep youre gonna have to farm some, but good news Practically Everything you need to farm is surrounded by other stuff you also need dam near All the time so farming really turns into exploring and quest doing which is Just what you want.... you dont really wanna spend  time farming instead of playing & they NAILED IT.  Also there are some cool ways you can farm in the not that distant future compared to the start of the game SO THAT is GREAT and Definitely means you'll be playing not farming. You craft in this game & they Nailed it! You can que your crafting so that you are free to do anything else and NOT SIT there and craft... GENIUS!!... The forest has it s own life and the civilians & wild life and the undead are GOING AT IT CONSTANTLY and so thats Very Cool and keeps the forest Alive & you on your toes! You can even use these forest rivalries to your advantage if youre a witty thinker.... Even when you finally bang out your first log cabin its pretty cool.... The build System once again Genius! Youre not locked in place when you want to place a anything from walls to floor so you can EASILY manipulate the camera or your vampire to get the angel or placement you need, also the visual way they portray it is slick it makes You feel like with blood magic You with your dark power is building instead of material plopping down. The ease you can switch weapons is Great it Definitely lends itself towards a plethora of discoverable combos. OH! Heres a MAJOR WIN for the developers and you will Totally get this if you enjoy crafting games. The workstations have MANY pockets! So instead of having to create 50 trunks to store all your CRAP..... you can just leave it at the workstation itll be there when you need it! GENIUS Such a time and energy saver! Forest fights and the Boss fights are Fun and Challenging i got lost in my castle building and had lvled up quite a bit past a boss i needed to go after but I dont think you can go but so far without doing the boss kills because slaying them unlocks stuff.   Overall the game is Spot ON... you FEEL like a risen VAmpire who is now on the charge to reclaim a land that has forgotten its kind, i think it will be even More fun to play with a friend & i wish i had 4  friends that would build with me because i bet the fights are NUTS with 4 Vampires! Also fighting is more about footwork and keeping an eye on ranged attackers which is Exactly what it would be if you only have a melee weapon, so foot work and sometimes the right weapon can really come into play which is cool and rewarding once you begin to put it all together. I have not discovered yet why they decided to have items that u can teleport with and items that you cant but thats the biggest head scratcher at the moment

I have a lot of games on Steam and also on my Xbox, I do not find many games engaging anymore, I buy games and play them once to check out cool graphics or just simply never play them. Its been a long time since I experienced this much fun playing any game. Everything is just perfect about this game for me, I love open world games where i can build bases, farm resources, slay enemies and level up or get better gear or magic skill and this game checks all the boxes. Thanks to developers of this game, I hope you realize you created masterpiece, Just advertise this game more, I had to dig real deep to find this game all by chance.
Best Vampire game out there! Really good survival crafting game with clear goals to grind for and bosses to fight to

Just finished Act III on brutal and this game is just awesome. Boss fights are unique and challenging, combat is extremely fluid and fun, and the building and crafting aspects feel polished and fleshed out. The game is a bit grindy though in the sense of always having enough mats for potions and co, but thats just a minor nitpick if anything.

This game is so good that I'm not even going to comment on it. What I want to know though; has anyone found something else that scratches this itch once you are done? I need more of these boss fights!
It's perfectly engaging when you want it to be and idle when you want it to be. The game is exactly what you make it out to be.

To be honest, it's hard to not like this game. The Dev made a awesome job with the game, the gameplay is fun and engaging, the world look alive with the environnement engaging with each other like a true immersive world.

The gameplay is simple enough to get the hang of it from the get go.

And not to mention the music track is beautiful in this game so far, I've not reach the end game yet but I can tell sometimes I just like to enjoy the beautiful music the game has to offer so far, can't wait to discover more.

One advice I would give though if you want to fully enjoy this game, is to play multiplayer for sure.
A wonderful PVE and pvp experience.  Highly recommend for those souls enthusiasts, POE lovers and survival fanatics alike

It is a bummer there isn't more story and cutscenes in this game, but the implied story from the snippets of boss flavor text is still interesting.
I adore the crafting, gathering, and fighting mechanics. Collecting materials and building the castle is wonderfully zen. The game is beautiful.
I'm not super excited about level being solely based on gear score, but the game makes it work.

V Rising is a top down survival game around vampires. I'm always surprised there aren't more vampire themed games out there, it's such a fun concept to make a game about. The survival aspect is really well done. You need to gather materials to upgrade everything. Your level is based on the level of your gear so if you are efficient hunting down materials, you can level up fairly quickly. You'll find bosses throughout the world which will unlock new things you can build. 

Building a base is also really intuitive and it's fun to make a castle in it. There are also some nice quality of life features to make your castle more efficient. You can make different rooms focused on certain aspects like a forging room to be faster and use fewer resources. It's all about being as efficient as possible!

Exploration and gathering materials is pretty fun. My main complaint is the controls can often times be confusing in the fast paced combat. I feel like the game was designed with a controller in mind and then the developers just kind of jammed together some keyboard controls as an after thought. I constantly found myself using the wrong abilities in a fight. Turning the camera mid fight is also a hassle. 

Overall though, I'm not even a big survival game fan, and I really enjoyed this one.
graphics are nice, gameplay is phenomenal. 10/10

I love this game because I love to build and building a castle is awesome there is so much to this game so much to do you can fish plant a garden build your castle make things capture a horse.. I dont want to give away to much I play on my own private server and I never run out of things to do and I dont have to worry about other people taking my stuff. I did have to put in a camera mod Retrocamera honestly im not sure why this abilty isnt base game if you dont have it you only look down not up and whats the use in building a castle and all the cool things in it if you cant see it very well. That I do recomend this game I havent had this much fun in a game in a very long time.

Adjust the world settings to make it not as grindy and slow, and you can have the epic vampire castle of your dreams. complete with fun bossfights, simple but good combat, and a huge world to explore.
Fun vampire game. A bit too grindy but that can be fixed with server settings. All in all a solid 8/10. Better with friends
Really fun. Not a game for everyone, but it really tickles the right parts of the brain for those who like these kinfd of games

played since it was in early access, jump again recently to try patch 1.1 and the new bosses.
the game is really good, highly recomended. way more fun to play with friends, but enjoyable nonetheless if playing solo. 
you can customice the server to your preference.  
building your castle is a blast.  and there like 100? bosses, 
if i have one complain is that the camera controls could be a little anoying. i want to create a panoramic  view of my castle T_T
I cant think of anything bad to say. Fantastic graphics. Fantastic art style. Fantastic gameplay. Castle building is fantastic. Everything is just fantastic.
Been playing on and off since day 1 and love it. Also love that it allows you to randomize the game within itself without mods.

unless you can get over getting dash checked with a dash that has 8 second cool down and deal with bosses having faster movement than you , and abilities which require a dash to dodge , i can not in good confidence recommended this game with the cooldowns it has legit 2 secs less on the veil cool down would mean the world , bosses like Grethel and Erwin are just beyond aids and have many times made me consider if these devs even know how to make a well paced boss fight. like why in the good reason is the veil cool down 8 seconds but everything else can move 10x faster than a vampire like come on . The veil cool down is genuinely just the really stupid and genially ruins the fun when i see 1 sec left on my dash and i die to a dash check attack cause oop i chose the wrong business day to use my dash  now ive gotta wait 10 business days to dash again , other let us move faster or decrease the cooldown on viel , unless those things are changed i can not recomend this game unless you like a machinic that is just punishing cause yes why not no other reason. will edit if opinion changes but for now Grethal and erwin are reason enough to uninstall cause of how slow a VAMPIRE is against a HUMAN

actually very good game, the gameplay and combat feels fluid, a lot of different spellcasting abilities without becoming overwhelming, the game progression is really fun, you don't really feel stuck (at least i didn't until the writing of this review)

i usually hate survival games, but this is really fun to play, the bosses are great (as a poe and souls enjoyer) and overall, really fun.
Great survival game. Controllers take some getting used to. Only played single players.
Very fun game! I love the mechanics and environment, as well as the gameplay.
great experience, tons of details that give life to to whole environment, easy craft system
Good format and art style.  Not too repetitive.  Can be a little challenging to control but not a steep curve before you get used to it.
If you enjoy survival crafting games and/or Vampires, this game is worth checking out.

Best co-op experience I've ever had! Also my new favourite survival crafting game. Looking forward to what the devs got cooking next!

EASY RECOMMEND

Great game, often on a free weekend. Picked it up for just £13 and ive already had 50 hours out of it. Easy to pick up and put down, some of the bosses are quite challenging solo. Only tried PVE so cant comment on the PVP element but I would think it plays well.
Great boss fights. Fun building and crafting. You can suck your friends.

honestly, i was skeptical at first, but I've grown to enjoy the game, and the building mechanics, along with the blood mechanic of it, very interesting. I'd highly Recommend giving it a try if you enjoy rpgs, the spells are also very fun, all around enjoyable
fantastic game. just coming from a great concert from real pro's. that that's v rising for me, a product from real pro's

I have played this game on and off and it is still amazing, one of my favorite survival games. The combat is snappy and responsive, the weapons are really fun, the boss mechanics are actually fun and the building is awesome.

I've played it twice through. Very fun game. Can get a little grindy in some parts with the refinement process, especially refining upwards (paper --> scrolls --> schematics) but overall it's a very fun, good gameplay progression loop game. 

Tight combat is the best part of this. Each V Blood (boss) is kind of like it's own real time puzzle that often requires adapting your strategy in the form of spells and abilities that are interchangeable. Each weapon provides a unique moveset and can be combined with different spells from different elements that you unlock throughout the game. 

Just a really fun time. 

Played on a 3070ti the first time through with zero issues or bugs.
Lady with robot legs wants to dominate me, but aside from that brilliant game
Some of the most fun I've had in the last few years. So polished. Great base building. Great combat. Great art style. Feels like the devs were very deliberate in their decisions. Absolute must play.

Honestly? I've been playing this game since EARLY EA.. I found it fantastic back then, but it was so basic I never dumped a lot of time into it. 

THEN 

We got so many QOL improvements, and new features and bosses that I was sucked back in. 1K Hours later and I found myself  having so much fun, yet so angry at the same time.

1.0 Release hasn't been any better. The crafting is fun, the increase from single floor castles to multi-floor castles is a major improvement. However, you are going to find yourself struggling against some of the bosses late game... and when I mean struggling I mean in a way that makes no sense. I'm nearly 10 level higher than one of the bosses and find myself getting slapped like i'm 20 below. 

It makes no sense what so ever and has caused a great many ALT-F4's. but that doesn't change my love for the game. It's different, it takes some time to get used to and learn the mechanics, but once you do you won't regret it.

Apparently the devs are amazing at listening to their player's feedback & made amazing changes. I think the extra DLC modpacks add a good bit to the game. The scalability increases w/ more players, including additional bosses & boss stages on the most difficult settings.

The inventory system is well thought out.

Some muliplayer elements I would consider to be too difficult & too easy at the same time.

8.9/10 would recommend to play . (Get it on sale tho)
This game is really good , specially the base building mechanism . The boss fights are super fun , the amount of skills and weapons are plenty . Although the endgame feels super empty , once you defeat final boss there isn't much to do , they could add some repeatable endgame content to make it even more fun and worthwhile.
Good game, the combat is very fun and the art style is beautiful, can't wait for the next game from this studio!

Amazing game. Perfectly designed, to the game world to mechanics to menus. 
It's survival craft, like Valheim. But the action is really really fun, it's a perfect mashup of genres. 10/10

Let me start telling you V Rising is an amazing game by itself with a lot of content and pretty fun to play either solo or with a friend, Took me almost 80 hours to complete the game including getting all achievements, the map is big enough to explore and the castle building plus the servant/prisoner system was pretty good too.

However just like any other game it also has it's own problems or flaws, to start it seems like the developers intended for V Rising to be a MMO service style game but somewhere down the development cycle the decided to make it also a single player experience but that seemed to be somewhat of an afterthought...

The main reason for my statement above stems from the fact that around the middle of the game the progression felt somewhat skewed (or somewhat forced) to be experienced by playing online with other people rather than actually rebalancing the single player mode on it's own, that and recent adjustements meant to balance the PvP experience are also efective in PvE, not that it affected me really but i'll say it just in case.

After the middle of the game I grew tired of having to fight bosses with gimmicks that gave a lot of trouble so I simply decided to skip like 6 bosses and went straight for the ones that gave me the recipes & crafting stations I needed to make better gear and then went after the earlier bosses I skipped wich died pretty easily after that, So progression seemed to be not balanced for single player or was not carefully taken into account because from my point of view there's no point in getting new gear when you no longer need it... (I played with a friend and we both complained about this while we played).

And last, the only real glaring flaw the game has is that technically speaking the game has no replay value after beating the last boss unless PvP is your thing, so once you beat the game and get the achievements there's nothing left to do content wise (aside from PvP).

Overall V Rising is a fun and purchase worthy game but take into account that once you beat it that's it, It's fun so I don't think you will regret purchasing it but it will last you maybe a few days if you dedicate yourself to play only this game.

Finally one question to the develpoers... Why do we have no BEDS has cosmetics for the castle!? I get it's supposed to be the coffin and that we are vampires in the game but THERE ARE BEDS already in the game... Why not get one even as a "useless" decoration for the castle?
Wanna be a vampire and spend more hours building your castle than actually do vampire stuff? This is the game for you.
Very much a Valheim clone (or is Valheim a V Rising clone?), however much more difficult.  I think it has ways to adjust the difficulty downward if desired.  Pretty fun though.
Vampire attac. Vampire protec. Vampire big succ. Get big power. Succ more. Get bigger power. Build castle. Break stuff. Fun clickings.

Devs put a lot of love into the game

While there is not a lot of character customization in terms of looks, your build is very customizable. You'll unlock more weapon types and and spell types and spell points as you progress. You can also unlock a station that lets you respec points.

On top of that, there's jewels you can spec into spells for more bonuses/perks, and there's a passive perk system

The castle building is great, and you can tell a lot of effort went into it. I've played a lot of survival craft RPGs, and this one does base building the best.

Downsides: not much I've seen so far other than devs are not adding content to the game anymore, opting for developing a new game in the same world.

Level is tied to gear progression, which is tied to a constant crafting and boss killing mill. Not bad, but I would prefer more kill enemies for exp to level up system.

NPCs react to each other, but that's pretty much fighting different factions. NPCs patrol roads all over the map, but no one questions or reacts to a castle built literally overnight. A siege mechanic by human NPCs would have been great.

This game is super fun! I love how many customization options there are, I love all the DLCs, and it's fun doing both solo and online gameplay. The only "downside" is that you can complete the entire questline pretty fast but it's 100% still worth the buy <3
Excellent arpg/survival crafting game with a great gameplay loop. Highly addicting and runs and feels great on handhelds. It's my favorite game on my Xbox rog ally x atm

I remember beating this game for the first time and going "Oh thank god that's over" but at 136 hours played who am I to complain. 

10/10 Much vampires, such castle building.

good game. enjoying to play it much. art of the game wonderful, dark and night times loves it. at the daytime daylight and harder to play in. but to me as it is normal for that is my life . problems with the sun and love to the moon.

V Rising is a pretty good action RPG/survival crafting game that suffers from balancing around the PvP leading to a much worse experience for the PvE content.

I'm not a big fan of survival crafting games, and I had no interest in playing this as a PvP game, so i played on a PvE world with one friend, and I also cranked up the resource gain and lowered crafting times. Without those changes the game would have felt incredibly grindy, but with them it wasn't too bad. 

The gameplay is pretty enjoyable until you start getting to the harder bosses which seem to be balanced completely differently to the player. Because V Rising is a PvP game as a well as a PvE game, the players abilities have to be balanced very carefully, which is fine for PvP. but when it comes to the PvE aspects the balance begins to clash. A lot of the later bosses are stupidly difficult because of AoE and projectile spam which you have to very carefully dodge because of the 8 second dodge cooldown. A single wasted dodge will almost certainly make to take extra damage on another attack that can't be avoided without the dodge or some other form of mobility. 

I also have some other minor nitpicks, such as the amount of crafting materials that are only used for a single thing, or how 3 of the transformations are almost completely worthless, and 2 are only used for a single thing. 

I enjoyed the game, but the further in into the game I got, the more frustating I found the games balancing and design.
Vampire World of Warcraft survival game with fancy castle building. 10/10

Pros and Cons 

Pros : 
   1. Extremely Fun!
  Great game for us Goths, Vampire nerds, Castlevania and RPG Fans. You can have multile games / saves / worlds, heavily customize your settings even during mid game from amout of loot dropped, wait times for merchants, building modifiers / supply costs, damage modifiers, teleporting with items usually not allowed, transforming into a bat with items not allowed to even starting off the game with "End Game Full Resources, Dracula level weapons and armor" (Great for trophy hunters like myself) 
   2. The fighting and spell mechanics are fun and easy to master and lots of different types of builds to try out even on one single character.
- THAT IS - IF you're running / hosting on your own dedicated server, instead of joining a game, not to mention you can also pull up the "Admin Console" if checkmarked in your settings on your own hosted server with the typical " ` " key and type in commands in game, for example : creating top tier jewels, weapons, armor, resouces, teleport to locations in an instant with no need for "waygates" Etc. So all in all, i say, very good game as you can cheese as heavily as you want and be in "God Mode".
   3. Worth getting the DLC's if you can afford to do so, it makes the game just a little bit much better in terms of some extra building visuals and a couple of transformantion options and again if you like Castlevania!! I'm hoping to puchase the rest of the DLC's as well in due time but id appreciate if the following would be addressed.

Cons (IMO - my personal Gripes, idk about anyone else..)
   1. I dont like the fact that we can only have about 800 floors of tiles for building, I'd like to make larger castles, i think it would be good at ...idk... lets say 1500 or 2000 tiles of flooring, without having the game break OFC and maybe up to 10 floors instead of 6?! just to get lost easier :) :P - and justify using teleport modules around you castle (maybe add more than just 4 teleports as well?!!?). Just a thought..
   2. The Constant Nerfing updates and patches :(
  I understand that from a PVP perspective and maybe boss fights the "dash" power has been nerfed countless times over time originally from "double dash" down to a 7 second window timer, and now as of todays new update, 8 seconds of down time?!?! That is extremely frusturating. As mentioned above, i feel that the fact you can heavily modify the settings of your own personal game / world / server settings, that the "double dash" should be back as an option or at least remove or modify the cooldown for said dash power in the settings. Maybe for PVP fights it gets triggered so that the timer is initicated then? IDK, but I'm not happy at all with that new patch update, no sir, not one bit I tell ya! **(insert crying sad face here)**
   3. Buiilding Mechanics should be updated and worked on a bit more.
  Specifically with the "Garden Paths, Bushes, gridlocking mishap / spacing situation. Thats a headache all in of its self when trying to make a nice consistent symetrical pattern :'(
   4. Ads and Bosses 
Fighting ads and bosses even when you're about 10 to 15 gear levels above them can*sometimes* still be HEAVILY challenging at times, even on normal or easy game settings (althhough i enjoy playing on brutal) but joining others games or starting a new game I still felt the struggle, and the GRIND to get more resoucres, in order to unlock this and that, to get that, to unlock w.e. etc etc, is brutally time consuming. NOT RECOMMENED for people who have other things to do in their day, it's definetley in aim to have you sitting here for hours on end barley getting anything done in game and in life LOL. Seems StunLock Studios really wants those "Playtime Numbers" to continue increasing..LOL, SNEAKY!! TISK TISK!
***One Last Thing!***
  Please, also ... give us the option to change our Charceters name the same way we can change our Characters Appearence from the mirror, Please.. I dislike the name I gave my Character and I'd really appreaciate it if whatever script or coding need to be implemented in order to so should be added, I'm sure  THAT ones not hard for the devs. Thanks!

This game feels like the smartest survival game. Everything about it works so well, and it's systems encourage a gameplay loop that is endlessly addicting. I've almost exclusively played this game on PVP multiplayer which, though a bit stressful, really brings out the best of this game's potential. 

I've played a lot of survival games, from V-Rising to Subnautica to Grounded to Minecwaft to Terraria to Conan's Exiles to The Forest to Windrose to Rust to DayZ to... the point is that only one of these games makes me want to do base-building intentionally. The combat is also a lot of fun, it feels extremely well balanced and no one character-build seems like the "meta" way to play, it's ultimately up to time investment, skill, and teamwork. High recommend, definitely join a server with 1.2+ item gathering to minimize time grinding.

Now that this game is legit modded, There is a mod for the camera to make it first person, and 3rd person by just the middle mouse wheel. Total game changer now. It was always a well built game but I am not a fan of top down games. This mod changes all that.

This game is a great little game to just play. But it seems that once you play through all the bosses and build a few castles....thats it. 

Unless you get into pvp heavily on some servers or they mod the game for new content i dont know if this will be around in a few years. 

But yeah worth the play and the price but it seems like it had potential to be like a Rust in a living breathing vampire world.

The most fun I have had in a while in a single PvE session.
Simply the best and comprehensive tutorial/guide that lead me through the game. It covers every boos, base function, base building technique, combat mechanics that start out simple but gradually ramp up to be more interesting and complex. Many weapons, styles, spells and passives to choose from. Range weapons are fun and the build paths are clear.
Base building is a blast.
After turning off portal restriction and tuning up resources/loot drop, the progression feel a lot smoother. Having to run back to base after looting just feels unnecessary in private session because there was no real threat unless very underleveled.
Would recommend the game to anyone likes base building, survival, exploration and vampire.

The game is fun, a bit grindy and the scale in which you can make your castle is so satisfying. Only things I don't really appreciate is how slow traveling can be even with the different forms. (I have only unlocked the wolf from for mobility.) As well as teleporting requiring you to drop half or most of your inventory, but besides that addictive and entertaining.
